title: Push: Add to End
description: Given an array and an element, use the `.push()` method to add the element to the end of the array. Return the modified array.
whatShouldHappen: Call .push() on the array, passing the element as the argument.
patternExplanation: .push() mutates the array by appending one or more elements to its end.

title: Pop: Remove from End
description: Given an array, use the `.pop()` method to remove the last element. Return the modified array.
patternExplanation: .pop() mutates the array by removing the very last element.

title: Unshift: Add to Beginning
description: Given an array and an element, use the `.unshift()` method to add the element to the very beginning of the array. Return the modified array.
whatShouldHappen: Call .unshift() on the array, passing the element as the argument.
patternExplanation: .unshift() mutates the array by adding one or more elements to the start, shifting existing elements to higher indices.

title: Shift: Remove from Beginning
description: Given an array, use the `.shift()` method to remove the first element. Return the modified array.
patternExplanation: .shift() mutates the array by removing the first element and shifting all subsequent elements down one index.

title: Simulate a Queue
description: Given an array representing a line of people (a queue) and a new person, simulate queue behavior. The new person joins the back of the line, and the person at the front of the line is processed and leaves. Return the array.
whatShouldHappen: Use .push() to add the new person to the end.
Use .shift() to remove the person at the front.

tests.label: empty queue edge case (shifts out the person just pushed)

patternExplanation: A Queue operates on a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) basis. In arrays, this is simulated using push (enqueue) and shift (dequeue).

title: Simulate a Stack
description: Given an array representing a stack of plates and a new plate, simulate stack behavior. The new plate is placed on top of the stack, and then the top plate is immediately removed (perhaps to serve it). Return the array. Note: In JavaScript arrays, the 'top' of the stack is the *end* of the array.
whatShouldHappen: Use .push() to add the new plate to the end.
Use .pop() to remove the plate at the end.
patternExplanation: A Stack operates on a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) basis. In arrays, this is simulated using push and pop.

title: Transfer Element
description: Given two arrays, `arr1` and `arr2`, remove the last element from `arr1` and add it to the beginning of `arr2`. Return an array containing both arrays: `[arr1, arr2]`.
whatShouldHappen: Use .pop() on arr1 to remove and return its last element.
Check if the popped element is undefined (in case arr1 is empty). If so, just return the arrays.
Use .unshift() on arr2, passing the popped element.
patternExplanation: Pop and Shift return the element they removed, which makes them perfect for handing data off to another array.
